baseline_evidence_output: The discussion focused on the effect of training on different languages. Professor B asked about the impact of training on one language and testing on another. PhD D explained that training on TI-digits and testing on TIMIT training led to a 10% error rate increase. When moving to multilingual data, the error rate increased by an additional 20%, showing a decline in performance across languages.
cc_evidence_output: The discussion about the effect of training on different languages involved Professor B and PhD D. Professor B mentioned that training on one language and testing on another language resulted in poor performance, as evidenced by a 10% error rate increase. PhD D noted that the model performed worse when transitioning to multilingual data, indicating that training on a single language does not generalize well to other languages. The key point was that training on one language and testing on another leads to significant performance degradation.
question: Summarize the discussion about the effect of training on different languages
reference_answer: Using multiple languages led to more diversity which potentially hindered the model performance. The professor was skeptical about discussing the results since they had not received any good ones so far.

cc_evidence_output: The Project Manager disapproved of the intelligent controller concept because the Marketing team raised concerns. question: Why did the project manager disapprove of the concept of intelligent controller?
reference_answer: The project manager put forward that the speech recognition wouldn't work when several persons wanted to change the channel at the same time, and the cost would surely rise if adding the two technologies. In response to that, the user interface designer said they would just cooperate with some research institute and the vocabulary was limited, so it wouldn't cost much. Then the project manager posed the language problem. The user interface designer answered they would have some language adaptations so the product could be used by people from all countries. Finally, there were still problems with the microphone and camera that were necessary for the two technologies.
